mechanism_narrative: Sustained job growth, as measured by non-farm payrolls, indicates a healthy and expanding economy. This boosts consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as investors anticipate stronger future performance.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows consistent positive month-over-month growth, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of such periods over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below a specific low threshold, it signals an accommodative monetary policy stance. This reduces borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, stimulating investment and spending, which supports equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS drops below 1.0% and remains there for at least three months, but the S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of subsequent periods over a 5-year span.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales combined with robust industrial production indicates broad economic strength, suggesting healthy consumer demand and productive capacity. This positive confluence drives corporate profits and stock prices higher, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.2%
failure_condition: If both RSAFS and INDPRO show positive growth as specified for two consecutive months, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered periods over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained rise in the unemployment rate during an economic downturn signals increasing economic stress and reduced consumer spending power. This leads to lower corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 returns as investors price in a weaker economic outlook.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months during a declared recession, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 30% of such instances over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in real GDP reflects overall economic expansion, which typically translates into higher corporate revenues and profits. This fundamental strength supports positive equity market performance, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 shows positive year-over-year growth for four consecutive quarters, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative on average over any 5-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: High and accelerating inflation erodes purchasing power and corporate margins, often prompting central bank tightening. This negatively impacts equity valuations by increasing discount rates and reducing future earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3.0% for three consecutive months, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 45% of subsequent periods over a 7-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts while interest rates are rising indicates a severe contraction in the housing market and broader economic activity. This signals a potential recession and leads to negative equity market returns as future growth prospects diminish.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 10% month-over-month AND FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25% month-over-month
failure_condition: If HOUST declines by more than 10% MoM concurrently with a FEDFUNDS rate hike of 0.25% or more, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 35% of such instances over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of already low unemployment, further declines in the unemployment rate become harder to achieve and may signal an overheating economy. This can lead to wage inflation and subsequent monetary tightening, which is negative for equities due to higher discount rates.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.0% AND UNRATE declines > 0.1 percentage points month-over-month
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: If UNRATE is below 4.0% and declines further by 0.1 percentage points or more in a month, but S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of subsequent periods over any 3-year rolling window.
